vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but incomplete. P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) and P1 (String of Nickels) are present. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) appear to be combined or missing in the observed pots. The yellow pot (P1) is largely devoid of visible foliage, suggesting a systemic loss of the primary specimen.

inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Mexican Mint)
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present (Pothos leaf and Rabbit anchor intact)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: New Introduction/Intervention (Single-leaf specimen in black pot, likely a propagation or seedling)

plant_audit.P2: No evidence of mint; pot appears to contain only soil/debris.
plant_audit.P3: Single Pothos leaf remains, stable posture.
plant_audit.P4: Single green leaf on a thin petiole; appears to be a new, fragile specimen.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp with visible perlite/white debris.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of eggshell fragments in P4, likely used for calcium or as a structural marker.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Minimal change; P3 leaf remains static.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t3: P4 leaf shows slight postural shift, leaning toward the light source.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: P4 leaf appears to be losing turgor pressure; slight drooping observed.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: P4 leaf remains in a low-turgor state; P3 leaf is stable.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: P4 leaf shows signs of recovery or stabilization; P3 remains unchanged.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: P3 is in a state of stasis (maintenance). P4 is a fragile, singular specimen showing signs of environmental stress (turgor fluctuation). P1 and P2 are effectively inactive/lost.
vision_report.anomalies: Eggshell fragments in P4 pot; absence of registered P1/P2 foliage.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of minimal activity. The primary Pothos (P3) is holding steady, while the new specimen (P4) is the only active biological variable. The yellow pot (P1) and the black pot (P2) show no signs of the registered species, indicating either dormancy or total loss. The environment is stable but lacks the diversity suggested by the registry.
